name: Ed Lewis
abstract: Ed Lewis (January 22, 1909 – September 18, 1985) was an American jazz trumpeter.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: A Salute to Count Basie

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: That's like Basie's band, wasn't but three of us in the band, trumpet section, when I joined there. Ed, Buck and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Count Basie
about_quotes.evidence: "In addition to Walter Page and Jimmy Rushing, there are Ed Lewis, my first trumpet player of long standing..."

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Ed Lewis, Sweets, and Emmett Berry, as I mentioned before, was the trumpet section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Young Snooky
about_quotes.evidence: I worked beside him for a long time when we played in Basie‘s band
about_quotes.doc_title: Young Snooky — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: Then we got Ed Lewis in his place... He was with Benny. Moten. Eddie was. He was a big shot with Benny... Basie, gave it to all four trumpet players before I got it. He started on this end and I happened to be sitting on this end. So Ed Lewis tried it, Al Killian tried it and I don't think it was Ha

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: He played a lot like Ed Lewis, Basie’s trumpet player, the kind of guy that there is nobody more comfortable than that, playing with Ed Lewis or Snooky Young or somebody like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
